triggerPath: Run pnpm audit --audit-level=low or run vulnerable Vitest UI/browser-mode tooling.
failureMode: The dev dependency tree contains vulnerable vitest <3.2.6.
consequence: Developer tooling dependency hygiene is red even though production dependencies are clean.
fix: Bump vitest and @vitest/coverage-v8 to >=3.2.6, refresh pnpm-lock.yaml, rerun audit, tests, and typecheck.

triggerPath: Push or deploy a commit with a vulnerable dependency that still passes existing CI gates.
failureMode: CI and deploy run license/test/build/file-size gates but no dependency audit.
consequence: Dependency advisories rely on a local manual command instead of the same gate discipline used elsewhere.
fix: Add an audit:deps script and run it in CI and deploy, or at least in CI before deploy is allowed.

failureMode: The main web chunk exceeds the configured 500 kB warning limit.
consequence: Initial app load and future feature additions are more likely to regress performance.
fix: Inspect the bundle graph, lazy-load optional heavy surfaces, and consider explicit manual chunks without raising the warning threshold.

triggerPath: Future theme work follows ADR-047 instead of the current light UI code.
failureMode: DECISIONS.md still describes dark chrome as accepted while tokens.css and index.html now implement light chrome.
consequence: Future agents can accidentally reintroduce the dark UI or mis-audit the theme.
fix: Add or amend an ADR to supersede ADR-047 with the current light-chrome decision.

triggerPath: Repeated compileJob calls with unchanged object arrays and fill settings.
failureMode: Pure-core compile logic contains module-level WeakMap state despite the repo policy banning module-level mutable variables.
consequence: Pure function reasoning and long-session testability are weaker unless this is made explicit.
fix: Pass an explicit compile cache from the caller, or write a narrow ADR exception documenting invalidation and tests.

triggerPath: Open a hand-edited or older .lf2 file that passes current shape validation but violates deeper invariants.
failureMode: deserializeProject normalizes raw JSON and then returns normalized as unknown as Project.
consequence: Future schema changes can accidentally be trusted by a broad cast instead of a typed construction path.
fix: Replace the final cast with typed builders for Project, Layer, and SceneObject variants, plus malformed .lf2 tests for cross-field invariants.

triggerPath: Editing trace, raster, compile, scene state, or laser state modules.
failureMode: Several high-risk files are physically large even though formal counted-code and raw-line gates pass.
consequence: Review and regression risk is higher in the modules most tied to image trace, raster output, job generation, and live laser control.
fix: Extract cohesive helpers during the next functional change in each large file; avoid unrelated refactor-only churn.
